title: Earthy Snappy Croatian Mushroom Pilaf
description: A hearty, earthy, snappy, indulgent Croatian dish featuring mushrooms, perfect for a donabe or other earthenware vessel. This high-protein-friendly recipe serves 4-6 people and can be prepared in under 45 minutes.

ingredients.name: Mushrooms (such as chanterelle or cremini)
ingredients.notes: Fresh or dried, sliced or whole.

ingredients.name: Dried thyme
ingredients.notes: Fresh thyme may be used instead.

instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together Greek yogurt and 2 tablespoons of butter until smooth and emulsified.
In a large donabe or earthenware vessel, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until translucent, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the white wine and cook until it has almost completely evaporated, stirring occasionally, about 5 minutes.
Add the water, mushroom broth, dried thyme, salt, and pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook, covered, for 10-15 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the mushrooms are tender.
Stir in the emulsified yogurt mixture and cook for 2-3 minutes, until heated through.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Garnish with chopped parsley and grated Parmesan cheese, if desired.
Serve hot, straight from the donabe.
